findings.description: The source code comments mark receive(), commit(), and send(res) with "(フレームワークが...)" to explicitly indicate these are framework-managed operations (not user-implemented code). The knowledge file drops this qualification: receive() loses its comment entirely, commit() becomes just "業務トランザクションをコミット", and send(res) becomes "応答電文を送信". Without the "(フレームワークが)" prefix, the distinction between framework-provided methods and user-implemented template methods is less clear in the code example.
findings.source_evidence: req = receive(); // (フレームワークが要求電文を受信) | commit(); // (フレームワークが業務トランザクションをコミットする。) | send(res); // (フレームワークが応答電文を送信)
